transcript_events.text: The scrollbar for the Supervisor tab in the toolbar for the web surface is on the wrong element; it should be on the .chat-output-box. Create a refine goal for this.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll create one narrowly scoped Refine goal for moving the Supervisor tab’s scrolling boundary to `.chat-output-box`, with browser verification in the acceptance criteria. I’ll first confirm the local goal-creation interface and current state so I don’t bypass supervision or duplicate an existing goal.

No existing goal matches this scrollbar ownership bug. I found related Supervisor UI goals, but they cover tab structure, input behavior, lifecycle controls, and status—not which element owns scrolling—so this is a distinct goal. I’m recording the implementation boundary and browser-level acceptance evidence without starting or promoting the goal.

Created Refine goal `00000SZ2PGMQR29F16GSKJ4000`: “Put the Supervisor chat scrollbar on `.chat-output-box`.”

It includes behavioral browser coverage and focused test requirements. Refine automatically claimed it as `in-progress` on branch `refine/00000SZ2PGMQR29F16GSKJ4000/round-2`; I did not manually start, merge, or approve it.

Created Refine goal `00000SZ2PGMQR29F16GSKJ4000`: “Put the Supervisor chat scrollbar on `.chat-output-box`.”

It includes behavioral browser coverage and focused test requirements. Refine automatically claimed it as `in-progress` on branch `refine/00000SZ2PGMQR29F16GSKJ4000/round-2`; I did not manually start, merge, or approve it.
Provider turn completed.
Sent 2 queued messages to the provider.

I’ll supervise the two live goals and the three queued retries through Refine’s own state and process surfaces. I’ll treat the newest snapshot as authoritative, preserve confirmation boundaries, and report any provider/authentication or recovery failure instead of masking it.

The latest evidence supersedes the earlier failed-count snapshot: the three retryable goals are queued as `todo`, while two workflow agents are live. I’m now checking the durable goal records, agent/process leases, and audit tail directly; I won’t infer health merely from the aggregate counts.

Refine has already replenished capacity: all three queued retries were claimed, so there are now five live workflow agents and zero queued work. The worker processes and their five goal-specific worktrees are present; no stall/loss evidence is visible yet. I’m continuing through review, merge, and cleanup rather than treating “claimed” as completion.

All five provider processes remain alive and are producing output; their logs show active repository/test commands rather than authentication or launch failures. The cross-process coordination goal is editing its isolated worktree; the other four are still in investigation/implementation. No confirmation request or destructive recovery action is warranted.

Two goals have cleared implementation, governance, and Quality and are now in `review`; three remain active. I’m auditing the review evidence and Refine’s normal transition commands before approving anything—`review` is not being treated as an automatic merge authorization.

The scrollbar goal was approved through Refine’s normal non-force path: its candidate merged cleanly, pushed to `origin/main`, and its worktree was cleaned only after merge. One separate goal—autonomous failed-Goal recovery—has now failed its validation and is explicitly visible as retryable; I’m inspecting that failure before deciding whether an evidence-backed retry is appropriate. The no-op Quality UX review remains unapproved because its own report says its prerequisites are not yet on `main`.

The failed recovery goal is intentionally parked behind the still-running shared-coordination goal; its retry would currently reproduce the same two evidence-integrity violations, so I am not cycling it. The two remaining workers are healthy and making progress in their isolated worktrees. One review is still held because it produced no implementation and explicitly requires the shared Quality evaluator first.

Both live goals remain within their configured 900-second stall window. Shared coordination is running the full library test set after format/diff checks; the Quality evaluator goal has recovered from an intermediate compile error and is continuing its implementation. No provider/authentication failure has occurred.

The two active agents surfaced ordinary test failures, not provider failures: coordination fixed a lowered-concurrency regression and is rerunning the full library suite; Quality is correcting its focused Quality tests. I’m leaving both under their managed processes and preserving their worktrees.

Shared coordination has completed implementation and committed candidate `bfdfc92`; Refine is now advancing it into independent governance/validation. The Quality goal is still resolving a focused retry-path regression. I will not retry the parked recovery goal until the coordination candidate is actually reviewed and merged.

Quality’s retry-path regression is now green after correcting runtime-root error handling; the agent is auditing removal of legacy command/settings paths before broader verification. Shared coordination remains in independent governance review.

Quality’s focused retry path now passes end to end with the failed candidate preserved; it has started the full library suite. Coordination governance is still actively checking recovery/process ownership paths and has not produced a verdict yet.

Independent governance rejected coordination candidate `bfdfc92` with three concrete issues: continuous owner-loss recovery, semantic conflict recheck after non-fast-forward state pushes, and missing concurrent failure-path tests. This is a legitimate in-scope retry—not a provider failure or a request for destructive authority—so I’m preserving the candidate, recording those findings as a new round, and requeueing it through Refine. The dependent recovery goal remains parked.

Refine accepted the new round but correctly refused `goal start` because the Goal is still `failed`; no worktree or merge was touched. I’m locating the existing failed-Goal recovery transition rather than bypassing the workflow state machine or editing durable JSON directly.

The new coordination round is now running in a fresh isolated round-7 worktree via Refine’s shared “last workflow state” recovery path; no durable JSON was edited directly. Current state is two active, zero queued, one parked failure, and one held review.

Quality’s full test run is green and it is doing a final quiet confirmation. Coordination round 7 is actively implementing the exact owner/provider and concurrent-push gaps from governance. State remains two active, zero queued, with the dependent recovery failure still deliberately parked.

Quality has also passed both normal and ignored full-workflow integration paths, plus final format/diff/legacy-path checks. It is at final review/commit; coordination remains active in its targeted fix.

Coordination has isolated the lost-owner defect to exact claim/lease evidence and is running the first targeted recovery test after patching. Quality is tightening test evidence after its green integration run; both remain under the managed workflow processes.

Both targeted gates are green so far: coordination’s live-provider and same-record conflict checks pass; Quality’s retry test and clippy pass, followed by a full-suite rerun after its final evidence changes. No merge or approval is being attempted before governance.

Coordination’s new genuinely concurrent same-record push test is green, and it is moving into cleanup/final verification. Quality’s integration tests remain green; the agent is doing a final shared-service ownership audit before committing.

The latest targeted fixes are green. Coordination’s one failed command was only an invalid combined test-filter invocation; the same tests passed when run separately. Quality added and passed a regression proving provider-reported success is rejected when supervised execution fails, then passed the full library suite.
Quality candidate `a40ab0e` is committed and has entered independent governance; coordination is still in implementation verification. Both remain unmerged and reviewable.

Coordination’s real two-daemon owner-loss, capacity-release, and synchronized push-race harnesses pass; format, diff, and all-target checks also pass. It is running the serial full library gate. Quality governance is auditing API/CLI/web ownership and retry semantics.

Coordination’s serial library and full-workflow gates are green; after a small final adjustment its focused recovery/conflict checks are green again. Quality governance’s targeted evidence-integrity and retry-path tests also pass.

A supplemental independent review caught an additional coordination crash window where `Goal=Failed` could persist before the claim became `Interrupted`; the active agent is fixing it before committing. Quality governance found no repository-evidence path under the searched ownership area (the search exited 1 for no matches) and is continuing its test audit.

The newly found crash window now has a passing regression that repairs a terminal Goal with a stranded running claim. Coordination is rerunning the complete serial gate; Quality governance is checking docs/intent provenance and cross-surface parity.

Quality governance rejected `a40ab0e` on two concrete issues: retry must preserve the failed round’s original Quality timing, and passing evidence must prove the worktree remained exactly at the candidate commit with no dirty mutation before/after execution. I’m preserving the candidate, recording those findings as a new round, and requeueing through the same shared recovery transition.

Coordination’s new ownerless-terminal test exposed an invalid test setup transition (`Todo→Cancelled/Failed`), not a provider or production failure; the agent is correcting the fixture. Quality round 3 cleanly merged its preserved candidate into the fresh isolated branch and is implementing only the two governance fixes.

Coordination’s corrected ownerless-terminal fixture passes and the full serial gate is still running. Quality has located existing Git cleanliness helpers and is wiring candidate/head/dirty checks into the shared Quality service rather than adding surface-specific logic.

Coordination’s 434-test serial gate is green and both production harnesses have completed; the agent is doing final scoped review before commit. Quality is updating the shared claim/context schema so retry timing is preserved durably.
Coordination candidate `ec0d336` is committed cleanly and has entered a fresh independent governance pass. Quality round 3 is still implementing its two scoped fixes.

Coordination governance is actively inspecting the new Git conflict and recovery state transitions. Quality has begun applying the timing and candidate-integrity changes in the shared service/claim path.

Coordination governance’s multi-instance and xtask production harnesses both pass; it is performing final candidate/goal evidence checks. Quality continues its scoped shared-core edits without touching main.

Quality’s current compile failure is explicit and local: the new `claim_with_quality_recovery` helper was placed in the trait implementation even though it is not a trait member. The agent is moving it to the inherent engine implementation; this is not being hidden as a provider failure. Coordination governance’s full-workflow rerun remains green.

Quality compiles cleanly after moving the helper to the correct engine implementation and is adding the two regression fixtures. Coordination governance is now checking Node identity/ownership semantics against the multi-instance harness.

Coordination governance found one remaining distributed-ownership defect: Node B can select or reconcile Node A’s claim because scheduling/recovery lacks an active-Node filter; the current two-daemon test uses the same default Node and misses it. I’m preserving `ec0d336`, adding a narrowly scoped distinct-Node round, and requeueing it through Refine.

Quality’s first multi-filter test invocation was syntactically invalid (exit 2); the same retry/context regressions pass when run as valid individual filters. Coordination round 8 is auditing all scheduler/reconciler Node checks against the preserved candidate before editing.

Quality’s focused behavior is correct, but the first full library run exposed six older fixtures that lacked the newly required candidate/failed-round evidence; the agent is updating those fixtures and race tests. Coordination round 8 remains active and unreviewed.

Quality’s remaining cancellation regression is failing because its helper process cannot see fixture Goal `GOAL-CANCEL`, so the managed Quality process never starts; the agent is correcting the test’s shared-state setup. Coordination compiles across all targets with the Node filters applied.
